skill.name: context-optimization
skill.description: Guides context compaction, observation masking, KV-cache optimization, partitioning, and context budget planning for long-running AI workflows.
skill.summary: Optimize AI context usage to reduce tokens, latency, and quality loss in long conversations.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mainly markdown code fences, context-engineering terminology, and cache-related prose rather than executable shell, network, or reconnaissance behavior. The only confirmed code concern is use of MD5 to generate short in-memory observation reference IDs, which is low risk because it is not used for authentication, encryption, or secret protection.

low_findings.title: Non-Cryptographic MD5 Reference IDs
low_findings.description: The Python utility imports hashlib and uses hashlib.md5 to create an 8-character observation reference ID. This is not used for authentication or encryption, but reference IDs should not be treated as secrets or access-control tokens.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly calls hashlib.md5, so the pattern is real. The risk is low because the ID is for an in-memory observation store and no evidence shows it protects secrets.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static findings for Ruby or shell backticks point to fenced Python examples in documentation. These lines are examples for compaction, masking, cache ordering, partitioning, and memory-aware optimization, not executable install or runtime commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown code blocks or surrounding documentation. No shell invocation, subprocess call, or external command execution is present there.

low_findings.title: Network Keyword False Positive
low_findings.description: The network finding points to text about KV-cache reuse across requests. Review found no HTTP library import, outbound endpoint, credential transfer, or network call in the cited documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe model request caching behavior, not code that sends data over a network. Targeted review found no requests, urllib, socket, or endpoint usage.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Terms Are Context Terminology
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance findings map to terms such as system prompt, cache metrics, observation references, and context categories. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment probing, or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines discuss AI context systems and cache metrics. They do not access host identity, local networks, process lists, or environment details.

dangerous_patterns.title: Short MD5-Based Identifier Generation
dangerous_patterns.description: The only confirmed risky code pattern is MD5-based generation of short reference IDs. Replace it with a random or collision-resistant identifier if references ever gate access to stored observations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The MD5 call is explicit, but the surrounding code indicates non-cryptographic use. The pattern becomes higher risk only if these IDs are used as authorization secrets.

content.value_statement: Long AI sessions can become expensive, slow, and less reliable when context grows without structure. This skill provides practical methods to compact, mask, cache, partition, and budget context for sustained work.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to apply compaction, observation masking, KV-cache optimization, and context partitioning.
Provides threshold guidance for triggering optimization near context limits.
Includes Python utility code for token estimates, message categorization, masking, and budget checks.
Describes cache-friendly prompt ordering for stable prefixes.
Defines metrics for evaluating token reduction, latency, cost, and quality impact.
Connects optimization choices to agent workflows and memory systems.
limitations: Token counting uses a rough character-based estimate, not model-specific tokenization.
Summarization utilities are heuristic examples and do not call an LLM.
Benchmark targets are guidance, not measured results from this package.
The skill does not provide a complete production context-management framework.

use_cases.title: Reduce Long-Session Cost
use_cases.description: Apply compaction and masking when a long AI conversation starts consuming too many tokens.

use_cases.title: Design Agent Context Budgets
use_cases.description: Allocate context across system prompts, tools, retrieved documents, message history, and buffer space.

use_cases.title: Improve Repeated Prompt Latency
use_cases.description: Reorder stable prompt elements to improve prefix caching and reduce repeated computation.

prompt_templates.title: Find Context Waste
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single long conversation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AI conversation for context waste. Identify which parts should stay, which can be summarized, and which can be removed.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Context Budget
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning token allocation for a workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a context budget for this workflow. Allocate space for system prompt, tools, retrieved documents, message history, outputs, and buffer.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Optimization Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Selecting techniques based on context composition.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this context profile and recommend compaction, observation masking, KV-cache optimization, partitioning, or a combined approach.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Optimization Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced implementation planning for an agent system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production context optimization plan with triggers, preserved information, masking rules, cache-stable prompt structure, metrics, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: A support agent conversation is near the context limit after many tool outputs.
output_examples.output: Recommended plan: keep the current issue, latest tool output, and user commitments. Summarize older turns. Replace resolved tool outputs with references and short conclusions.

output_examples.input: A batch workflow repeats the same instructions for many similar requests.
output_examples.output: Recommended plan: move stable instructions to the prefix, keep variable request details at the end, remove timestamps from shared prompt text, and track cache hit rate.

output_examples.input: A research agent must process several large documents.
output_examples.output: Recommended plan: partition documents across focused sub-agents, summarize each result, validate coverage, and merge only the final evidence needed for synthesis.

best_practices: Measure token use and quality before adding optimization logic.
Preserve task goals, user preferences, recent turns, and active decisions during compaction.
Keep stable prompt content first so repeated requests can benefit from prefix caching.
anti_patterns: Do not compact system instructions or active task state.
Do not mask observations that are still needed for current reasoning.
Do not add partitioning overhead when context limits are not causing problems.

faq.question: What does this skill optimize?
faq.answer: It optimizes how AI workflows use limited context through compaction, masking, caching, partitioning, and budget planning.

faq.question: Does it increase a model context window?
faq.answer: No. It helps use the available context more efficiently, but it does not change the model limit.

faq.question: Can it reduce token costs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can reduce repeated or low-value context, which may lower token usage in long workflows.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python utility script with example functions for estimation, masking, budgeting, and cache metrics.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production use as-is?
faq.answer: Not by itself. The included script is demonstrative and should be adapted with real tokenizers, storage, tests, and monitoring.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
